mappings.reason: Implementing a firewall on servers enables packet filtering on host network interfaces, addressing a subset of the network filtering scope (host-level vs infrastructure-level).
Implementing a firewall on end-user devices enables packet filtering on host network interfaces, addressing a subset of the network filtering scope.
Network-based URL filtering enforces dynamic packet filtering for web traffic, addressing a specialized protocol-specific subset of the requirement.
Performing traffic filtering between network segments directly implements dynamic packet filtering on the network infrastructure, satisfying the primary objective.
Host-based intrusion prevention solutions block malicious traffic at the host level, addressing a threat-based subset of the packet filtering requirement.
Network intrusion prevention solutions block malicious traffic at the network level, addressing a threat-based subset of the packet filtering requirement.
Application layer filtering performs dynamic packet inspection and filtering at Layer 7, addressing a specialized subset of the filtering mechanism.
